question_text: Salt water can be separated into salt and water using distillation. Why is distillation needed instead of simple filtering?
correct_answer: Salt dissolves in water to form a solution, so filtering cannot separate it because the salt particles are too small and dispersed throughout the liquid. Distillation works by heating the water until it evaporates and condenses back into a separate container, leaving the dissolved salt behind.
explanation: When salt dissolves, it breaks down into particles too small to be caught by filter paper. Distillation uses the different boiling points of water and salt to separate them: water boils and evaporates while salt remains as a solid.
